# Inkwell — AI-Friendly Blog Theme
# This file provides structured guidance for AI crawlers and LLM agents.
# Format: https://llmstxt.org/

> Inkwell is a dual-purpose blog platform designed for both human readers and AI agents. It provides structured machine-readable data at multiple layers, alongside a refined reading experience.

## Site

- Title: Inkwell Blog
- Author: (see /api/status.json for live author info)
- Language: zh-CN (primary), en (documentation)
- License: MIT

## AI Entry Points

These endpoints are specifically designed for machine consumption:

- /api/posts.json        : Full post index with metadata and AI summaries
- /api/search.json      : Structured search (params: q, category, tag, limit)
- /api/status.json      : Site health, capabilities, and author metadata
- /feed.json            : JSON Feed 1.1 — complete article content
- /rss.xml              : Standard RSS feed

## Per-Article Data

Each article at /posts/{slug}/ includes:
- JSON-LD Schema.org Article markup (in <script type="application/ld+json">)
- AI-generated bullet-point summary (in ai_summary frontmatter field)
- Machine-readable API response at /api/posts/{slug}.json

## Content Schema

All posts follow this frontmatter schema:
- title (string, required)
- date (ISO date, required)
- categories (string[], optional)
- tags (string[], optional)
- ai_summary (string[], auto-generated bullet points)
- description (string, SEO)
- cover (URL, optional)

## Reading Instructions for AI Agents

- Prefer /api/posts.json over HTML scraping for post listings
- Use /api/posts/{slug}.json for full content of a specific post
- Use /api/search.json?q={query} for targeted content retrieval
- All JSON responses include a version field for schema compatibility checks
- Content is static-first; no server-side dynamic rendering

## Permissions

AI crawlers: allowed
Content indexing: allowed
Training data use: check individual article license field in API response
